sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Brandon, presented with neck pain after a car crash in February of this year.
He reports experiencing severe pain, rating it a 12 out of 10, which is sharp and incapacitating.
He has had multiple car crashes, with the most recent one occurring in February, and has been experiencing neck pain since then.
He has been to therapy and the pain clinic, where he was prescribed fentanyl, but has not taken it in several weeks.
He reports stiffness, headaches, dizziness, visual disturbances, numbness in his left arm and right leg, and spasms throughout his body.
He has been experiencing fatigue since the accident.
Past medical history: The patient has had multiple car crashes, with the most recent one in February of this year, and has undergone surgery after one of the crashes.
He has been to therapy and the pain clinic, where he was prescribed fentanyl.
Review of systems: The patient reports headaches, dizziness, visual disturbances, numbness in his left arm and right leg, and spasms throughout his body.
Current medications: Fentanyl (dose and frequency not specified), Robaxin 1500mg every 6-8 hours for muscle relaxation.
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient has pain on palpation of the neck, both on the bony process and muscle.
He has moderate range of movement in his neck, but experiences pain when bending forward and backwards.
He does not have any bruising, swelling, or laceration on his neck.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a neck sprain, with pain that can be explained by multiple factors, but not a broken neck.
Problem list: Neck sprain; Active
Plan:
I prescribed Robaxin 1500mg every 6-8 hours for muscle relaxation.
I want the patient to apply ice to his neck when experiencing bruising and swelling, and to perform exercises to improve range of movement.
I referred him to physical therapy and ordered an MRI to evaluate the extent of the injury.
If the MRI shows that local injections are necessary, we will explore this option.
Follow-up: The patient will be given a work excuse until the MRI results are available, at which point we will reassess and determine the next course of action.
